volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 3.2.847c - Section 4.3.924)

sections.label: Section 3.2.847c
sections.translation: Go , by all means .

sections.label: Section 3.2.848
sections.translation: I ’ ll go in-doors , then .

sections.label: Section 3.2.848b
sections.translation: Stop , please ; let me see whether the dog —

sections.label: Section 3.2.848c
sections.translation: Very well then , look .

sections.label: Section 3.2.849_850
sections.original: Est ! abi , canis . est ! abin diérecta ? abin hinc in malam crucem ?
sections.translation: Be off and be hanged ! ’ st , won ’ t you be off to utter perdition with you ? What , do you still linger ? ’ st , away with you from here !

sections.label: Section 3.2.851b
sections.translation: There ’ s no danger . You only move on . It ’ s as gentle [*] as a woman in childbed . You may boldly step in-doors wherever you like . I ’ m going hence to the Forum .

sections.label: Section 3.2.853b
sections.translation: You ’ ve acted obligingly . Good speed to you . ( Exit SIMO .) Tranio , come , make that dog move away from the door inside ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.855
sections.translation: although it isn ’ t to be feared .

sections.label: Section 3.2.855b
sections.original: Quin tu illam aspice ut placide accubat ;
sections.translation: Nay but ( pointing ) , you look at it , how gently it lies . Unless you ’ d like yourself to appear troublesome and cowardly —

sections.label: Section 3.2.856b
sections.translation: Very well , just as you like .

sections.label: Section 3.2.857
sections.translation: Follow me this way then .

sections.label: Section 3.2.857b
sections.original: Equidem haud usquam a pedibus apscedam tuis .—
sections.translation: For my part , I shall not move in any direction from your feet .

sections.label: Section 4.1.858
sections.original: Serví qui , quom culpá carent , tamén malum metuont ,
sections.translation: Servants who , though they are free from fault , still stand in awe of punishment , those same are wont to be serviceable to their masters .

sections.label: Section 4.1.860
sections.original: nam illí qui nil metuónt postquam sunt málum meriti ,
sections.translation: But those who fear nothing , after they have merited punishment , hit upon foolish plans for themselves : they exercise themselves in running ; they take to flight . But , if they are caught , they acquire from punishment a hoard , which by good means they cannot .

sections.label: Section 4.1.864_865
sections.translation: They increase from a very little , and from that they lay by a treasure . The resolution that ’ s in my mind is to be determined to be on my guard against punishment , before my back comes to lose its state of soundness . As hitherto it has been , so does it become my hide still to be , without a bruise , and such that I should decline its being beaten .

sections.label: Section 4.1.870
sections.original: si huíc imperábo , probé tectum habébo ,
sections.translation: If I have any control over it , I shall keep it well covered up [*] . When punishment is being showered down on others , don ’ t let it be showered down on me . But as servants wish their master to be , such is he wont to be . He is good to the good , bad to the bad . But now at our house at home there do live so many rogues ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.875
sections.translation: lavish of their property [*] , bearers of stripes . When they are called to go fetch their master , I shan ’ t go ; don ’ t be plaguing me ; I know where you are hurrying off to , is the reply . Now , faith , you mule , you ’ re longing to go to pasturage out of doors [*] . With better deserts , this advantage have I reaped from them , and , in consequence , I have come from home .

sections.label: Section 4.1.881
sections.original: hóc die crástini quóm erus rescíverit ,
sections.translation: I alone , out of so many servants , am going to fetch my master . When , to-morrow , my master comes to know this , in the morning he will chastise them with bull ’ s-hide spoils . In fine , I care less for their backs than for my own . Much rather shall they be bull ’ s-hide-scourged than I be rope-scourged [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.885
sections.translation: Hold you and stop this instant . Phaniscus ! look round , I say !

sections.label: Section 4.2.886a
sections.translation: Don ’ t be annoying to me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.887
sections.translation: Do see how scornful the monkey is !

sections.label: Section 4.2.887a
sections.translation: Are you going to stop this instant , you dirty parasite ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.888
sections.translation: How am I a parasite ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.888b
sections.original: Égo enim dicam : cíbo perduci pótis es quovis .
sections.translation: Why , I ’ ll tell you : you can be drawn anywhere by victuals .

sections.label: Section 4.2.889
sections.original: Mihi súm , libet esse . quid íd curas ?
sections.translation: I am so for myself ; I choose to be . Why do you trouble yourself about it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.890
sections.original: Ferócem facís , quia te érus amat .
sections.translation: Do you give yourself airs , because your master ’ s so fond of you ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.890b
sections.translation: O dear , my eyes do ache [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.891b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.891c
sections.translation: Because the smoke ’ s so troublesome .

sections.label: Section 4.2.892
sections.original: Tace sís , faber , qui cúdere soles plúmbeos nummos .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , will you , you clever workman , who are in the habit of coining money out of lead [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.893
sections.original: Nón potes tu cógere me , ut tíbi male dicam .
sections.translation: You cannot compel me to be abusive to you . My master knows me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.894b
sections.translation: Why , really , his own pillow [*] he ought to know , for resting on when drunk .

sections.label: Section 4.2.896
sections.original: Si sóbrius sis , male nón dicas .
sections.translation: If you were sober , you wouldn ’ t be abusive .

sections.label: Section 4.2.896b
sections.original: Tibi obtémperem , cum tu míhi nequeas ?
sections.translation: Am I to give heed to you , when you won ’ t to me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.897
sections.original: Át tu mecum , péssime , ito advérsus ; quaeso hercle abstine
sections.translation: But , you rascal , you come along with me to fetch him .

sections.label: Section 4.2.898
sections.translation: Troth now , Phaniscus , prithee , do leave off [*] talking about these matters .

sections.label: Section 4.2.898b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so , and knock at the door . ( Knocks at the door of the house of THEUROPIDES .) Hallo there ! is there any person here to protect this door from a most serious injury ? ( Knocking again .) Is any one , is any one , I say , coming out here and going to open it ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.900
sections.original: foribús defendat ? écquis has aperít foris ?
sections.translation: Why , really , no one comes out here . Just as befits such worthless fellows , so they are . But on that account , I ’ ve the more need to be cautious that no one may come out and use me ill .

sections.label: Section 4.3.904
sections.translation: What ’ s your opinion of this bargain ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.904b
sections.translation: I am quite delighted .

sections.label: Section 4.3.905
sections.translation: Does it seem to you to have been bought too dear ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.905b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m sure that I never anywhere saw a house thrown away , this one only excepted .

sections.label: Section 4.3.906b
sections.translation: Does it please you , then ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.907
sections.original: Ecquid placeant me rogas ? immo hercle vero perplacent .
sections.translation: Does it please me , do you ask me ? Why yes , upon my faith , it really does please me very much .

sections.label: Section 4.3.908
sections.translation: What a fine set of rooms for the women ! What a porch !

sections.label: Section 4.3.908b
sections.translation: Exceedingly fine . For my part , I don ’ t think that there is any Porch larger than this in the public buildings .

sections.label: Section 4.3.910
sections.original: Quin ego ipse et Philolaches in publico omnis porticus
sections.translation: Why , I myself and Philolaches have taken the measure sure of all the porches in the public buildings .

sections.label: Section 4.3.911b
sections.translation: Well , what then ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.911c
sections.translation: This is far larger than all of them .

sections.label: Section 4.3.912
sections.original: Di immortales , mercimoni lepidi . si hercle nunc ferat
sections.translation: Immortal Gods — a splendid bargain ! On my word , if he were now to offer six great talents of silver , ready money , for it , I would never take it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.914b
sections.original: Si hercle accipere cupies , ego numquam sinam .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , if you were inclined to take it , I would never let you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.915
sections.original: Bene res nostra conlocata est istoc mercimonio .
sections.translation: My money has been well invested upon this purchase .

sections.label: Section 4.3.916
sections.original: Me suasore atque impulsore id factum audacter dicito ,
sections.translation: Boldly confess that by my advice and prompting it was done , who urged him to take up the money of the Banker upon interest , which we paid this person by way of deposit .

sections.label: Section 4.3.918b
sections.translation: You ’ ve saved the whole ship . Eighty minae [*] , you say , are owing for it ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.919b
sections.translation: Not a coin more .

sections.label: Section 4.3.920
sections.translation: He may have it to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.3.920b
sections.original: Ita enim vero , ne qua causa subsiet .
sections.translation: By all means so , that there may be no dispute arising ; or else pay them over to me , I ’ ll then pay them over to him .

sections.label: Section 4.3.922
sections.original: At enim ne quid captioni mihi sit , si dederim tibi .
sections.translation: But still , don ’ t let there be any taking me in , if I do give them to you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.923
sections.original: Egone te ioculo modo ausim dicto aut facto fallere ?
sections.translation: Could I venture to deceive you in deed or word even in jest only ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.924
sections.original: Egone aps te ausim non cavere , ne quid committam tibi ?
sections.translation: Could I venture not to be on my guard against you , so as not to trust anything to you ?
